nodes.label: AI as creative springboard for lesson planning
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for teacher's own ideas
nodes.grounding_span: thinking outside of the box when coming up with lesson plans

nodes.label: AI as time-saving collaborator for non-core tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as crutch that stifles creativity
nodes.grounding_span: if I'm short on time, I would rather put my ego aside and get some help

nodes.label: AI as enhancement to teacher's expertise
nodes.grounding_span: I really want what I receive from the AI to be an enhancement to what I'm doing rather than the core

nodes.label: AI for student engagement and motivation
nodes.grounding_span: The lyrics went over really well with him and because they engaged him so much

nodes.label: Preserving personal teaching style and creativity
nodes.label_negative: AI-driven instruction that dilutes teacher's style
nodes.grounding_span: I want my own style and personality to shine

nodes.label: Student benefit and learning outcomes
nodes.grounding_span: giving my student the best possible instruction

nodes.label: Professional pride and identity
nodes.grounding_span: I still pride myself as a good teacher

nodes.label: Efficiency and time management
nodes.grounding_span: I didn't really have the time to come up with a set of lyrics

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for engaging student
Mixed feelings about AI integration
nodes.grounding_span: there are times I feel mixed about integrating AI into my approach

nodes.label: Resistance to AI in core instruction
nodes.grounding_span: I don't need the AI to handle telling me how to actually instruct the students

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through back-and-forth with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I just keep asking it to tweak things or change things until I'm satisfied

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting for AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I don't want to feel like I'm simply giving my students AI based lessons
